_method: Constraint propagation on 12 low-confidence V16 roots
_key_finding: All 4 k-initial roots decompose as k-prefix + confirmed root. k is a TIER-2 PREFIX, not part of the root.

roots.structural_analysis: k(prefix) + ar(iure). Confirmed: ar=iure. k is tier-2 prefix.
roots.note: If k is empty determinant: kar=iure. If k=t: kar=ture (frankincense). qokar = cum+iure or cum+ture.

roots.structural_analysis: s(prefix) + aiin. s is tier-2 prefix (sub/sine/super?). aiin is root #1 (1430x).
roots.prefix_combos: Always standalone (129x as saiin). No other prefix before s.
roots.note: saiin never takes another prefix before it, suggesting s IS the prefix. Resolving s-prefix value would unlock this and all s-initial words.

roots.structural_analysis: k(prefix) + ain. Confirmed: ain best_guess=in (medium). k is tier-2 prefix.
roots.note: Depends on resolving both k-prefix and ain root values.

roots.structural_analysis: k(prefix) + edy. Confirmed: edy best_guess=locis (medium). k is tier-2 prefix.
roots.note: Parallels otedy=tere et. If edy=ere: kedy = k+ere.

roots.structural_analysis: k(prefix) + y. Confirmed: y=in. k is tier-2 prefix.
roots.note: Very short root. qoky=coque (confirmed monolithic logogram) uses same glyphs but is NOT k+y decomposition.

roots.structural_analysis: a + l(medial=l, from glyphs.json) + y. NOT a+ly or al+y.
roots.decomposition: Monolithic: a(a) + l(l, medial) + y(i/ium) = ali/alium
roots.note: Uses medial_l=l rule from glyphs.json. Allium (garlic) is one of the most common pharma ingredients. Already found in v9_ALIUM (aim=alium). 116x frequency fits a common ingredient.

roots.structural_analysis: i + r(re). Part of the Xr paradigm: ar=iure, or=hiera.
roots.note: In pharma context, ire could mean go/proceed in instructions. dair=109x would be d(in)+ir(ire)=in ire. Xr paradigm: ar=iure, or=hiera, ir=ire.
os(bone/mouth) is medically relevant. But through standard glyph rules o!=o and s!=s. Could be a logogram like many short roots.

roots.structural_analysis: o + iin. iin is a morphological unit (ura/ure/ur/are).
roots.note: Parallels aiin(ura), but with o- prefix/initial. If o=a: aura fits both linguistically and medically.

roots.structural_analysis: d + aiin appearing AS A ROOT after other prefixes. NOT the standalone word daiin(847x).
roots.prefix_combos: ok+daiin(60 as odaiin), qo+daiin(44 as qodaiin), ch+daiin(46 as chodaiin)
roots.note: Strongly confirmed by odaiin=edura logogram. In pharma context: dura=hard (hardening instructions). qodaiin = cum+dura.

roots.structural_analysis: eee + y. Triple-e followed by y. Variant of eey(730x)?

latin_candidates.path: scribal variant of eey=mense with extra vowel

roots.note: Most likely a scribal/orthographic variant of eey. The VMS often shows e-doubling for vowel length. If eey=mense, eeey may encode the same or similar word with different vowel quantity.

roots.structural_analysis: ee + o. Double-e followed by o. Variant of eo(181x)?

latin_candidates.path: scribal variant of eo with extra vowel

roots.note: Likely scribal variant of eo(181x). o= confirmed logogram for ac. So eeo could be ee+ac. If ee=oo/oe: eeo=oo+ac or oe+ac.

key_breakthrough.finding: k is a TIER-2 PREFIX, not part of the root
key_breakthrough.evidence: ALL 4 k-initial unknown roots (kar, kain, kedy, ky) decompose perfectly into k + a confirmed/medium-confidence root (ar, ain, edy, y). This also applies to medium-confidence k-roots: kaiin=k+aiin, keey=k+eey, keedy=k+eedy.
key_breakthrough.implication: Resolving the Latin value of k-prefix would immediately unlock ALL k-initial roots (kar, kain, kedy, ky, kaiin, keey, keedy, kal, key, kol, kody, etc.)
k_candidates: k = empty determinant (like ok without the o)
k = t (V15 brute-force optimal, but contradicts kol=cis)
k = c (standard K&A mapping, consistent with kol=cis)
